The update logic fails to maintain the invariant that display_name must always mirror name when the name field changes.

The profile update logic recomputes full_name only when last_name changes, so first_name updates leave full_name stale and out of sync.

Updating the email does not reset the user's verified state or clear the cached greeting, so stale greeting data persists after an email change.

The cache is never invalidated when a product is updated, so get_product can return stale copies after write operations.

The catalog updates the database but never invalidates the cached product entry, so get_product() can return stale cached data after an update.

The module caches _default_host at import time, so get_host() returns a stale value instead of reading the current settings dynamically.

The client eagerly caches the config at import time, so reset_config() only clears the source config module while client.py keeps using a stale _client_config reference.

The client captures a stale config dictionary at import time, so after reset_config() the handler still reads old values through the client module.

The queue function uses a mutable default list argument, causing tasks to persist across calls instead of creating a fresh queue when none is provided.

The worker function uses a mutable default argument for seen, causing task IDs to persist across calls and incorrectly skip valid tasks in later batches.

The decorator uses a module-level mutable default list, causing all decorated scheduler functions to append to the same shared history instead of maintaining independent histories.

The batch processor emits only one event after the loop instead of one event per item, so event counts do not match processed items.

The retry loop never stops after a successful send and the clear_log utility is unimplemented, so the sender keeps retrying unnecessarily and cannot clear its log.

The pipeline retries the sender without stopping after a successful attempt, so each message can be sent multiple times and accumulate 3-4 duplicates.

The order flow re-raises wallet payment failures without releasing the previously reserved inventory, leaving stock incorrectly locked after a failed charge.

The rate-limit comparison is off by one: it uses count > limit, which delays blocking until after the limit is exceeded instead of blocking at the limit boundary.
